response.justification: The candidate is a capable, versatile senior engineer with genuine low-latency and event-driven systems experience, but he does not match this role's core profile. His hands-on C++ is brief and dated (roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project); his recent decade is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no evidence of MQTT, industrial protocols, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle practice, and his cloud exposure is AWS rather than Azure. For a C++-centric industrial automation position requiring secure engineering per IEC 62443, the gap on the primary competencies is too large.
strengths: Real high-performance systems experience: low-latency asynchronous I/O, fibers, and caching on micro-services handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering craftsmanship culture — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — plus open-source authorship of test tooling
Proven mentor and educator through workshops, refactoring dojos, and multi-day courses, matching the role's mentoring expectation
concerns: C++/STL experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; primary recent stacks are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No demonstrated MQTT, Modbus/OPC UA, IEC 62443, or SDL/secure-coding background required for industrial cybersecurity work
No industrial automation or IoT domain depth, and cloud experience is AWS rather than the preferred Azure
